title: Chyren-OmegA Architecture Graph
description: Network map of architecture layers, verification mechanics, runtime split, and evidence discipline.

nodes.label: Chyren
nodes.description: Sovereign intelligence orchestrator integrating governance, identity, verification, and persistence controls.

nodes.label: AEGIS
nodes.description: Governance shell that constrains policy and risk before execution.

nodes.label: AEON
nodes.description: Identity and task-state layer coordinating continuity across runs.

nodes.label: ADCCL
nodes.description: Verification gate that scores drafts and enforces pass/fail routing before persistence.

nodes.label: MYELIN
nodes.description: Memory substrate receiving writes only after verification passes.

nodes.label: Cortex-Medulla Split
nodes.description: Python orchestration and Rust runtime separation improving modular control and runtime rigor.

nodes.label: Chiral Invariant χ
nodes.description: Mathematical decision guardrail for acceptance versus reject-repair.

nodes.label: Proof Pack v0.2
nodes.description: Versioned evidence with run logs, snapshots, and trend charts.

nodes.label: Novelty Discipline
nodes.description: Claim framing method requiring mechanism, reproduction command, artifacts, and limits.

edges.description: Chyren depends on AEGIS to impose governance constraints before any execution path can proceed, preventing policy bypass during orchestration and persistence decisions.
Chyren uses AEON identity state to maintain coherent task continuity across turns and runs, so verification can evaluate outputs in the correct context.
Chyren routes every candidate output through ADCCL, ensuring verification is mandatory and not optional before any memory or ledger action occurs.
ADCCL governs MYELIN write permissions by allowing only pass-scored outputs to persist while failed outputs are redirected to reject or repair.
Chiral Invariant χ informs ADCCL threshold behavior by formalizing orientation and residual alignment into an explicit decision boundary for accept versus reject outcomes.
Cortex-Medulla Split supports Chyren by separating planning/orchestration responsibilities from high-performance runtime components, reducing coupling and clarifying failure domains.
Proof Pack v0.2 provides measurable execution evidence for Chyren by storing run status logs, metrics snapshots, and trend visualizations tied to reproducible commands.
Proof Pack v0.2 enables Novelty Discipline by forcing architecture claims to link to concrete metrics and logs rather than unverified language or anecdotal assertions.

Novelty Discipline evaluates Chyren claims against reproducibility and boundary-condition standards, distinguishing implemented differentiators from hypotheses that still require external replication.
